# md2u

> A lightweight, open-source, cross-platform Markdown previewer for Windows and Linux.

## What it does

md2u renders .md files the way they were meant to look — the same rendering engine as Obsidian. Double-click a .md file and see clean, formatted output instead of raw symbols.

Built with Tauri. Free. No account required. GPL v3.

## Key features

- Obsidian-style Markdown rendering
- 10 built-in CSS themes (Obsidian, GitHub, Swiss, Ink, Multi-Column, Grump, and more) + Custom CSS
- Live reload — auto-updates on file save
- Drag & drop or CLI: `md2u yourfile.md`
- Find bar (Ctrl+F / Cmd+F) with Whole words, Case sensitive, Regex
- Mermaid diagrams (flowcharts, sequence diagrams, Gantt charts)
- Obsidian-style Callouts (`[!NOTE]`, `[!WARNING]`, etc.)
- One-click export: HTML (with CDN), PDF (A4 paginated), DOCX, MD, OPML

## Platform support

- Windows: x64, ARM64 (.exe / .msi)
- Linux: Debian/Ubuntu (.deb), Fedora/RHEL (.rpm)
- macOS: planned

- English: https://md2u.stanwu.org/
- 繁體中文: https://md2u.stanwu.org/zh-TW/
- 简体中文: https://md2u.stanwu.org/zh-CN/
- 日本語: https://md2u.stanwu.org/ja/

## Why md2u exists

Most Markdown tools are either macOS-only (Marked2), too heavy (VS Code, Obsidian, Typora), closed-source, or paid. md2u fills the gap: a dedicated previewer for Windows and Linux that opens fast, stays out of the way, and renders files correctly.
